title: Release Notes & Frequently Asked Questions | Respiratory Illnesses | CDC

Why are there two data sources for hospitalization rates? 
We use two primary data sources for respiratory hospitalization rates: RESP-NET and the National Healthcare Safety Network (NHSN). By using both RESP-NET and NHSN, we aim to provide the most comprehensive view of hospitalization trends related to respiratory illnesses. 
As of November 1, 2024, the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is requiring hospitals to report information regarding COVID-19, influenza, and RSV hospitalizations, bed capacity, and occupancy to NHSN. NHSN has been added to the Illness Severity page because it has data from almost every hospital in each U.S. state. However, because these data are only required as of November 1, 2024, we also include data from RESP-NET to show longer-term trends from select hospitals across 14 states. 
RESP-NET will continue to be used on the People Most Impacted page because it provides detailed demographic breakdowns. 
Our goal is to present the most complete data regarding respiratory illness hospitalizations. By using these two sources, we ensure that users have access to both histo
